name: Chorizo Quiche in Cornmeal Crust
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the crust, sift together cornmeal, flour, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and black pepper. Cut in shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
2. Add water, 1 tablespoon at a time, stirring lightly until the mixture forms a ball.
3. Turn out onto a lightly floured board and roll dough to a 13-inch circle. Fit loosely into a 9-inch pie plate or quiche pan.
4. Fold the edge under and flute. Arrange cheese slices on the bottom of the crust.
5. Fry chorizo until cooked and crumbly. Drain off fat. Sprinkle chorizo over the cheese, then top with green onions.
6. Lightly beat eggs in a bowl, stir in whipping cream and season to taste with salt and pepper. Pour over the cheese and chorizo mixture.
7. Place in the bottom rack of the oven and bake at 450°F for 15 minutes.
8. Reduce heat to 350°F and bake an additional 25 to 30 minutes.
9. Let stand 10 minutes before cutting.
